decision: Keep nil collaborator substitution behavior consistent with default service contracts
reason: LSP issues in the scoped markets files came from seams where nil options or zero-value test doubles changed behavior unexpectedly. Centralizing defaulting and predictable fallback/error behavior preserves substitutability without changing market semantics.
impact: Service option setters now normalize nil collaborators to defaults, domain sentinels keep stable interface types, and test doubles assert predictable zero-value behavior.
